frames.name: Presidential Agency Frame
frames.description: Presents Trump as the primary decision-maker who acted preemptively based on intelligence about Iranian intentions, emphasizing his leadership role
frames.emphasis: Trump's claim that he 'forced Israel's hand' rather than being pressured by Israel, and his assessment that Iran was going to attack first

frames.name: Administration Contradiction Frame
frames.description: Highlights the discrepancy between Trump's statements and Secretary of State Rubio's earlier comments about Israel's role in prompting US action
frames.emphasis: The apparent contradiction between Trump saying he forced Israel's hand versus Rubio's statement that Israel was planning to strike first

frames.name: Military Success Frame
frames.description: Focuses on the claimed effectiveness of the strikes and Iran's weakened military capabilities
frames.emphasis: Trump's assertions that 'virtually everything they have has been knocked out' and Iran's reduced missile capabilities

frames.name: Regional Escalation Frame
frames.description: Covers Iran's strikes against neutral countries and the broader regional conflict
frames.emphasis: Iran's attacks on previously neutral countries and the surprise this caused

frames.name: Congressional Oversight Frame
frames.description: Focuses on legislative branch demands for briefings and potential votes on war powers
frames.emphasis: Congressional Democrats demanding answers and potential votes on presidential war powers

outlierPerspectives: Trump's claim that Iran killed '35,000 protesters' in recent weeks (only mentioned in YouTube ABC News coverage)
Spain's refusal to allow US use of jointly operated bases for Iran strikes
DHS investigation into possible 'Iranian sleeper cells'
Trump's statement about US munition stockpiles allowing wars to be fought 'forever'
missingContext: Timeline of events leading to the strikes - when negotiations occurred, what intelligence prompted the assessment
Clarification of what specific 'negotiations with lunatics' Trump referenced
Details about the legal/constitutional basis for the strikes without congressional authorization
Verification of casualty claims and damage assessments from independent sources
Background on the US-Spain base agreement and its terms
Context about which 'neutral countries' Iran allegedly struck and verification of these claims
